header.choose.tile.rainbow.player: {player} declares a rainbow and can get another tile
header.two.for.one.you: Two for one: discard another tile
header.two.for.one.get.you: Two for one: take a tile
header.two.for.one.player: {player} can discard 2 tiles to get one
header.discard.tile.you: You have to discard one tile
header.family.you: Play a second tile to start your family
help.tile.description.animals: 8 Animal families (8 colors) with 11 tiles per Animal:
help.tile.objectives.description: <action>Place</action>, <action>exchange</action>, and <action>expand Animal families</action>. At the end of the game, each family in front of you scores 1 point per tile. Aim to create families of at least <bold>7 tiles to earn bonus points</bold>.
help.tile.discard: This tile has been discarded. When a player has 6 or more tiles at the end of a turn, they must discard a tile.
help.seven.token.description: Each time one of your families <bold>reaches at least 7 tiles</bold> (while expanding), take a 7 token, <bold>it earns 1 point</bold>.
help.double.seven.token.description:
If you are <bold>the first to achieve two families with at least 7 tiles each</bold>, also take the 77 token, <bold>it earns 2 points</bold>.
This action triggers the <bold>END OF THE GAME</bold>.
help.actions.description: You may perform <bold>as many actions as you wish</bold> or are able to. <bold>Each action can be repeated</bold>, except for the Special Action.
help.actions.1.description:
From your rack, place a family of <bold>at least 2 identical Animal tiles</bold> in front of you, visible to all players.
One or more Wild tiles (Clovers) can be used to start a family. However, a family cannot be made up entirely of Clovers.
help.actions.2.description:
From your rack, add one or more tiles (Animal or Clover) to expand a family already placed in front of you.
A family must consist of only one type of Animal, with optional Clovers.
help.actions.3.description: Exchange one of your Animal families with that of another player. Both families must have <bold>the exact same number of tiles</bold>.
help.actions.4.description: You may retrieve a Clover (Wild tile) from a family placed <bold>in front of you or another player</bold>. From your rack, take an <bold>Animal tile that matches the family</bold>. Replace the Clover with this tile and place the Clover on your rack
help.actions.5.description: <bold>Once per turn only! Discard 2 tiles face-up</bold> from your rack. Then, <bold>draw a new tile</bold>, visible or hidden.
history.flip.tile: {player} flips a tile and reveals <0>{color, select, 1{an orange} 2{a pink} 3{a green} 4{a purple} 5{a grey} 6{a red} 7{a blue} 8{a maroon} other{a clover}} tile</0>
history.discard.tile: {player} discards <0>{color, select, 1{an orange} 2{a pink} 3{a green} 4{a purple} 5{a grey} 6{a red} 7{a blue} 8{a maroon} other{a clover}} tile</0>
history.start.family: {player} starts a family of <0>{color, select, 1{orange} 2{pink} 3{green} 4{purple} 5{grey} 6{red} 7{blue} other{maroon}} tiles</0>
history.exchange.family: {player} exchanges a family of <0>{color, select, 1{orange} 2{pink} 3{green} 4{purple} 5{grey} 6{red} 7{blue} other{maroon}} tiles</0> for a family of <0>{otherColor, select, 1{orange} 2{pink} 3{green} 4{purple} 5{grey} 6{red} 7{blue} other{maroon}} tiles</0> with {otherName}
history.seven.token: {player} takes a <0>7 token</0> and wins 1 point
history.double.seven.token: {player} takes the <0>77 token</0> and wins 2 points
rules.lastTurn.you: You triggered the end of the game.
rules.lastTurn.lastPlayer: {player} triggered the end of the game.
rules.lastTurn.text: Each other player takes one final turn.
discard.rules.title: Discard a tile
discard.rules.description: When, at the end of your turn, you have 6 or more tiles, you must discard one and only one tile.
tuto.step.1: In <bold>Double Seven</bold>, your goal is to <bold>place and expand</bold> families of Animals.
tuto.step.2: Here's your <bold>rack</bold>. Each player starts with 3 randomly chosen tiles.
tuto.step.3: On your turn, you start by drawing 2 tiles.
tuto.step.4:
You can draw face-down tiles, or choose a face-up tile.
Draw this tile.
tuto.step.6:
After drawing, if you have at least 5 <bold>all different</bold> tiles (and no Clovers), you can declare "Rainbow."
You then reveal your tiles to your opponents, and you can draw an additional tile.
Declare your Rainbow using the button in the header.
tuto.step.8: After drawing, you can perform <bold>as many actions as you like, in any order</bold>.
tuto.step.9: The first possible action is to <bold>start a new family</bold>: take 2 identical tiles from your hand and place them in front of your rack to start a family.
tuto.step.10: You have no more possible actions, you must <bold>Pass</bold> to give the hand to your opponent.
tuto.step.11: Your opponent has taken his turn. He has started this family.
tuto.step.14:
Now it's your action phase.
Start by creating a new family with your two identical tiles, just like in the first round.
tuto.step.15: The second possible action is to <bold>expand a family</bold>: take a tile from your rack and add it to the sequence of one of your families of the same animal.
tuto.step.16:
The third possible action is to <bold>exchange a family</bold> with an opponent's family. The two families exchanged must have <bold>the same number of tiles</bold>.
Exchange your family of two tiles with your opponent's (drag and drop it onto the other family).
tuto.step.17: Thanks to the exchange you just made, you can once again expand your family. Do it.
tuto.step.18:
The fourth possible action is "2 vs. 1": discard your two remaining tiles from the draw pile, in order to draw another tile instead.
<italic>The "2 vs. 1" action is only possible once per turn.</italic>
tuto.step.19:
You've drawn a Clover! Clovers are rare and can be used to expand <bold>any family</bold>.
Warning: The 5th possible action is to <bold>retrieve a Clover</bold>. On their turn, <bold>any player</bold> can retrieve a Clover by swapping it with a tile of the correct family.
tuto.step.20: Play your Clover to expand the family of your choice.
tuto.step.21: When you have no more tiles on your rack, you must pass your hand by announcing: "Empty!" This is very interesting, because you will draw <bold>3 tiles instead of 2</bold> on your next turn!
tuto.step.22:
When a player places <bold>the 7th tile</bold> of a family, they take a <seven/> token.
The first player with two <seven/> tokens also takes the <seven/><seven/> token and <bold>triggers the end of the game</bold>: the other players each take one last turn.
tuto.step.23:
<bold>Scoring:</bold>
• Each tile in front of you is worth 1 point.
• Each <seven/> token is worth 1 point.
• The <seven/><seven/> token is worth 2 points.
The player with the most points wins.
In the event of a tie, the player with the <seven/><seven/> token wins.
tuto.step.24: Now it's your turn, good luck!
